name: Truffles
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the milk in the brandy and set aside.
2. In a heavy saucepan, combine the chestnut puree, sugar, and vanilla over medium-high heat, stirring constantly. Cook until thickened (approximately 6-10 minutes).
3. Reduce heat slightly. Whisk in the milk/brandy mixture and cook until thick enough to form peaks (approximately 5 minutes).
4. Transfer to a mixing bowl and allow to cool for 20 minutes.
5. Line a baking sheet with waxed paper. Form the mixture into small, truffle-sized balls and lay out on the sheet. If you find it difficult to make the balls, allow the mixture to cool a little longer. Place the sheet in the fridge and chill.
6. Melt 4 oz of the chocolate in a double boiler over hot water (do not boil). Heat until it reaches 120°F on a candy thermometer. For those without a thermometer, this is just after the chocolate melts.
7. Remove from heat and add the remaining chocolate. Stir until shiny.
8. Place the chocolate in a shallow bowl and roll the truffles in the chocolate. If your first attempt is not successful, you may need to melt more chocolate. Truffles will keep in the fridge for a week and in the freezer for six months.
